resources.string: Stay on top of your assets across Solana, Ethereum, Base, Sui, and Tezos. Native coins and tokens, unified in a single, clean dashboard.
Simply add your public addresses to start tracking instantly. We never ask for seed phrases or private keys, ensuring your portfolio remains secure and private.
Take control of your data. Configure custom RPC endpoints and use your own API keys for the fastest, most reliable updates on every network.
Let\'s start by providing a name for your main account.
Please provide a name for your main account. Don\'t worry, you can change it later.
You can also set an initial balance for this account, if you want.
Select a name for your main account
Please enter a name for the account
Please provide at least one category.
Please enter the %1$s of the transaction
From this screen, you can add a new transaction and categorize it by amount, cause, date, extra budget, relevant account, and category.
Income: Money coming into your account.
Expense: Money spent on purchases or payments.
Needs: Essential expenses required for daily living.
Wants: Non-essential spending for enjoyment or lifestyle.

Flag this transaction as an \"Extra Budget\" entry if you wish to exclude the corresponding amount from your monthly budget calculations.\nThis may apply to your initial savings or any unplanned income or expenses that you consider unrelated to your regular monthly financial planning.

Keep in mind that the Budget section represents an ideal theoretical scenario, assuming all transactions are accounted for and budgets are strictly followed.\nTransactions marked as extra-budget are excluded from these calculations, meaning spending beyond the allocated amount may affect actual availability.\n

Use Extra Budget carefully: overusing it may lead to an inaccurate perception of your financial situation, making it harder to stay on track with your goals.\nAnd Always check the Overview balance before making financial decisions!
Are you sure you want to delete this account? This operation is irreversible, you will lose all data related to it.
Are you sure you want to delete this transaction? This operation is irreversible, you will lose all data related to it.

This represents your monthly balance, derived from the Savings Budget you\'ve established, combined with the remaining amounts from the other two categories.\nShould you surpass the budget allocated to those categories, the excess is deducted from your Savings Budget, and then from your Global Savings.\nTransactions marked as EXTRA BUDGET are not included in this calculation.
The 50/30/20 rule is a simple budgeting framework that helps manage personal finances by dividing one\'s income into three categories:
Essentials like housing, food, utilities, transportation, insurance.
Non-essential spending like dining out, entertainment, shopping, hobbies, and vacations.
Emergency funds, investments, retirement contributions, extra debt payments, and financial goals.

This method promotes financial balance by ensuring necessities are covered, lifestyle choices are enjoyed in moderation, and future security is prioritized.\nIt’s a flexible guideline that can be adjusted based on individual circumstances.
It works best when all transactions are accounted for and spending stays within planned limits.

This budgeting system is a helpful guide, not a guarantee.\nThe calculations and values displayed in the Budget section represent an ideal theoretical scenario where all transactions are accounted for, and budgets are strictly followed.\nBudget calculations include all transactions that are not flagged as extra-budget. As a result, extra-budget transactions and spending beyond the allocated amount may significantly distort the actual availability for a given category.
Ultimately, it’s just a tool.\nReal financial decisions should always be based on your own judgment, awareness, and priorities.
No provider available for this chain
Estimated number of days until funds are exhausted based on current expenses
Shows your spending risk based on current activity
Tap on the graph to see more details
The chart reflects how much of your income you’ve saved each month.
Do you spend more if you earn more?
Here you can see the month-by-month difference between income and expenses. Swipe the graph to reveal older months.
Estimate your total savings for the next 3, 6, and 12 months, based on the average savings of the past few months.
Needs and/or Wants exceeded the 100% of your income.
The bars show how much you spent on \'wants\' each month.
The bars show how much you spent on \'needs\' each month.
Compare your ideal budget distribution with your actual spending for this month
It looks like we don\’t have enough data to show the chart yet.

Welcome to Axii! These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of our mobile application and related services ("Service"). By using Axii, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.
By downloading, installing, or using Axii, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms.
You must be at least 18 years old to use Axii. By using the Service, you represent that you meet this requirement.
Axii is a personal finance management application designed to help users budget their income using percentage-based allocations. It does not provide financial, investment, tax, or legal advice.

- You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials (if applicable). - You agree to use Axii only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. - You acknowledge that all financial decisions made using Axii are at your own risk.
Your use of Axii is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.

- No Financial Advice: Axii is an informational tool and does not provide professional financial services. We do not guarantee the accuracy of financial projections. - No Liability: We are not responsible for any financial losses, damages, or inaccuracies resulting from the use of the Service. - No Warranty: Axii is provided "as is" and "as available," without warranties of any kind.
We reserve the right to modify, update, or discontinue the Service at any time, with or without notice.
We may suspend or terminate your access to Axii if you violate these Terms or engage in any fraudulent or abusive behavior.
These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Spain.
If you have any questions about these Terms, you can contact us at dev@mariorobertofortunato.com.
By continuing to use Axii, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to these Terms.\n\nThank you for using Axii!
For any inquiries, feel free to reach out to us via email.
Start your {{ sub_offer_duration }} trial, then {{ total_price_and_per_month }}.
No browser installed. Link could not be opened.
Link invalid. Could not be opened.
Axii shows people how much they should be able to spend in each category, based on the 50/30/20 budgeting rule and the assumption that:
All their income and expenses are correctly recorded.
They follow the plan and don’t overspend.
They don’t have any surprise or untracked transactions.
But in real life, people might:
Spend extra outside the plan (e.g., emergencies, impulse buys).
Mislabel something as “extra-budget” when it isn’t (or vice versa).
Rely too much on what Axii says they can spend, without checking their actual bank balance.
So the risk is that someone sees a green number in the Budget or Overview and thinks: \"Cool, I can spend this!\"\n…even though that number might not reflect the true state of their finances.
Why this issue happens in Axii:

Axii calculates your budget based on a rule (like 50% for needs, 30% for wants, 20% for savings) and on the transactions you’ve entered.\nIt then shows you an “ideal” version of your finances: "If you earn this much, and spend this way, then you should have this much available."\nBut in real life:
Not all spending follows the plan – Life is messy. People sometimes spend more than planned, or spend money from one category for another.
Some transactions are missing – Users may forget to log cash payments, small transfers, or incoming income.
Extra-budget transactions distort the numbers – If you mark something as “extra-budget,” Axii ignores it for budget calculations, even though it still affects your actual balance.
Timing differences – Budgets are monthly, but real spending can spill over months, or not line up neatly.
Manual categorization mistakes – If you mislabel a transaction, the numbers get skewed.
Axii still shows you values in the Budget and Overview based on the information it has—which might not be complete or reflect your current reality.\nThat’s why we say:
Axii works best when used carefully, consistently, and with full context. Otherwise, the numbers might look “right” but actually mislead you.
Let\'s break this down using an example and explain why the Budget section could give a misleading picture.
Needs: According to Axii, you have $500 available for needs (because of the 50% rule).
You’ve spent $250 on regular needs, which is under budget—no issue here.

But $500 of your needs spending is classified as "extra-budget", so Axii doesn\’t include this in its main budget calculations. This means that Axii\’s budget calculation only counts your $250 regular needs, but not the extra $500 spent outside the normal budget.
Why the Budget Section Might Be Misleading:
Axii will show that you\’re well within your $500 needs budget, because it\’s only counting the $250 you spent and ignoring the extra $500 as “extra-budget.”
In reality, you’ve spent $750 on needs, which is $250 over the planned 50% budget.
The Budget section tells you that you have $250 left for needs, but you actually need to account for the extra $500 that’s outside of the system.
You might also overspend in wants or savings, but since Axii is focusing on the standard 50/30/20 rule, it doesn\'t reflect the real situation when extra-budget expenses happen.

In this example, the Budget section would look like you\'re under budget, but in reality, you\'re overspending because some of your expenses were classified as “extra-budget” and weren\’t included in the calculations.\nThis could give you a false sense of control over your spending, leading you to think you\'re within budget when you\'re actually overspending.
Looks like spending got a little ahead of the plan, and your current balance isn\’t enough to support your full monthly budget.

To keep things accurate, we\'ve activated Survival Mode: instead of using your income as the base, we\'re now calculating your budget based on the funds you have left.\nThis way, what you see better reflects your real situation. Survival Mode will turn off automatically once your balance can fully cover your planned expenses and savings.
